finding: D. JUVENILE FORM 10% of tubules involved + gross hepatic fibrosis + bile duct proliferation

differentials.name: disease appears at 6 months to 5 years of age
differentials.raw: disease appears at 6 months to 5 years of age

differentials.name: portal venous hypertension
differentials.raw: portal venous hypertension (5-13 years of age)

differentials.name: Potter facies: low-set + flattened ears, short + snubbed nose, deep eye creases, micrognathia
differentials.raw: Potter facies: low-set + flattened ears, short + snubbed nose, deep eye creases, micrognathia

differentials.name: Chromosome studies to determine if other malformations present
differentials.raw: Chromosome studies to determine if other malformations present (eg, trisomy 13 / 18)

differentials.name: Nonintervention for fetal distress > 24 weeks if severe oligohydramnios present
differentials.raw: Nonintervention for fetal distress > 24 weeks if severe oligohydramnios present

context: √  portal hypertension: √  hepatosplenomegaly √  ascites Prognosis:  death from portal hypertension Radiography √  abdominal distension √  gas-filled bowel loops deviated centrally √  small bell-shaped thorax → severe pulmonary hypoplasia √  pneumothorax / pneumomediastinum IVP: √  bilateral gross smooth renal enlargement √  faint nephrogram + blotchy opacification on initial images √  increasingly dense nephrogram √  poor visualization of collecting system √  "sunburst nephrogram" = striated nephrogram with persistent radiating opaque streaks (= collecting ducts) on delayed images √  prominen
